message.content:
Explore the src/optimizer/ directory thoroughly to understand the C++ OR-Tools optimizer implementation. Document:
1. All files and their purposes
2. Build system (CMakeLists.txt, Makefile)
3. IPC protocol implementation (stdin/stdout NDJSON)
4. Constraint handling (hard vs soft)
5. TypeScript fallback optimizer (if any)
6. Compare against spec 002-architecture.md and 004-domain-services.md Section 4.3
7. Identify what's implemented vs missing

content.text: I'll explore the src/optimizer/ directory thoroughly to understand the C++ OR-Tools optimizer implementation. Let me start by examining the directory structure and then dive into the details.

    95→## 2.2 Component Responsibilities
    96→
    97→### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)
    98→
    99→| Responsibility | Implementation |
   100→|----------------|----------------|
   101→| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
   102→| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
   103→| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
   104→| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
   105→| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
   106→| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
   107→| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
   108→| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
   109→| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
   110→| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |
   111→
   112→### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)
   113→
   114→| Responsibility | Implementation |
   115→|----------------|----------------|
   116→| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
   117→| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
   118→| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
   119→| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
   120→| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
   121→| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
   122→| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |
   123→
   124→### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service
   125→
   126→| Responsibility | Implementation |
   127→|----------------|----------------|
   128→| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
   129→| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
   130→| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
   131→| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
   132→| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
   133→| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |
   134→
   135→### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers
   136→
   137→| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
   138→|--------|----------------|-------------|
   139→| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
   140→| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
   141→| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
   142→| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
   143→| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
   144→| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
   145→| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
   146→| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |
   147→
   148→### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary
   149→
   150→| Responsibility | Implementation |
   151→|----------------|----------------|
   152→| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
   153→| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
   154→| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
   155→| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
   156→| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
   157→| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |

   161→## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162→
   163→### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164→
   165→```
   166→06:00 │ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) → File Watcher detects
   167→06:05 │ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168→06:10 │ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169→06:15 │ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170→06:30 │ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171→      │   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172→      │   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173→07:00 │ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174→07:00 │ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175→      │   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176→      │   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177→08:00 │ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178→16:00 │ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179→22:00 │ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180→```
   181→
   182→### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183→
   184→```
   185→Monday 06:00  │ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186→              │   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187→              │   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188→Monday 06:30  │ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189→              │   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190→              │   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191→Monday 07:00  │ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192→              │   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193→              │   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194→Monday 08:00  │ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195→Tuesday       │ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196→              │   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197→              │   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198→Wednesday    │ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199→Friday       │ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200→```
   201→
   202→### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203→
   204→```
   205→User Action  │ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206→             │   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207→             │   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208→API Call     │ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209→             │   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210→             │   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211→             │   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212→             │   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213→Post-Publish │ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214→             │ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215→```
   216→
   217→### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218→
   219→```
   220→User Action  │ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221→             │   - System forks from current published baseline
   222→             │   - New scenario_id assigned
   223→Editing      │ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224→             │   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225→             │   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226→Comparison   │ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227→             │   - System materializes both scenarios
   228→             │   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229→             │   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230→Promotion    │ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231→             │   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232→             │   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233→```
   234→

   237→## 2.4 C++ Optimizer Stream Protocol
   238→
   239→### 2.4.1 Stream Architecture

   243→│                 │ ─────────────────────────────► │                 │

   248→│                 │ ◄───────────────────────────── │                 │
   249→│                 │    stderr (logs, metrics)      │                 │

   433→
   434→### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435→
   436→**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437→
   438→```typescript
   439→// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440→class OptimizerWorker {
   441→  private process: ChildProcess;
   442→  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443→  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444→  
   445→  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446→  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447→  
   448→  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449→    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450→      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451→      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452→      
   453→      // Handle stream events
   454→      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455→        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456→          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457→          
   458→          try {
   459→            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460→            
   461→            switch (msg.type) {
   462→              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463→                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464→                break;
   465→                
   466→              case 'PROGRESS':
   467→                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468→                break;
   469→                
   470→              case 'RESULT':
   471→                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472→                resolve(msg);
   473→                break;
   474→                
   475→              case 'ERROR':
   476→                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477→                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478→                break;
   479→            }
   480→          } catch (parseError) {
   481→            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482→          }
   483→        }
   484→      });
   485→      
   486→      // Handle process crash
   487→      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488→        if (code !== 0) {
   489→          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490→          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491→            type: 'ERROR',
   492→            request_id: request.request_id,
   493→            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494→            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495→            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496→          }));
   497→        }
   498→      });
   499→      
   500→      // Send request
   501→      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502→    });
   503→  }
   504→  
   505→  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506→    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507→    
   508→    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509→      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510→      
   511→      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512→        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513→        
   514→        // Kill hung process
   515→        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516→        
   517→        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518→          type: 'ERROR',
   519→          request_id: requestId,
   520→          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521→          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522→          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523→        }));
   524→      }
   525→    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526→  }
   527→  
   528→  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529→    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530→      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531→      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532→    }
   533→  }
   534→}
   535→
   536→class OptimizerError extends Error {
   537→  readonly errorCode: string;
   538→  readonly requestId: string;
   539→  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540→  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541→  
   542→  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543→    super(msg.error_message);
   544→    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545→    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546→    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547→    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548→    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549→  }
   550→}
   551→```
   552→
   553→**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554→
   555→The C++ binary must:
   556→1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557→2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558→3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559→4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560→
   561→```cpp
   562→// Signal handler example (C++)
   563→void signalHandler(int signal) {
   564→    nlohmann::json error;
   565→    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566→    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567→    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568→    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569→    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570→    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571→    
   572→    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573→    std::cout.flush();
   574→    
   575→    _exit(128 + signal);
   576→}
   577→```
   578→
   579→The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580→
   581→### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582→
   583→The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584→
   585→### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586→
   587→```typescript
   588→interface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589→  period: {
   590→    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591→    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592→  };
   593→  
   594→  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595→  track_capacity: {
   596→    depot_id: string;
   597→    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598→    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599→    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600→    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601→  }[];
   602→  
   603→  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604→  labor_capacity: {
   605→    depot_id: string;
   606→    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607→    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608→    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609→    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610→  }[];
   611→  
   612→  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613→  material_capacity: {
   614→    depot_id: string;
   615→    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616→    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617→    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618→    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619→  }[];
   620→  
   621→  // Constraints from tactical model
   622→  blocked_trains: {
   623→    train_id: string;
   624→    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625→    reason: string;
   626→  }[];
   627→}
   628→
   629→class ConstraintAggregationService {
   630→  /**
   631→   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632→   */
   633→  async generateEnvelope(
   634→    quarterStart: Date,
   635→    quarterEnd: Date,
   636→    depotIds: string[]
   637→  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638→    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639→    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640→      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641→    );
   642→    
   643→    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644→    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645→      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646→    );
   647→    
   648→    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649→    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650→      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651→    );
   652→    
   653→    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654→    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655→      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656→    );
   657→    
   658→    return {
   659→      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660→      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661→      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662→      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663→      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664→    };
   665→  }
   666→  
   667→  /**
   668→   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669→   */
   670→  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671→    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672→    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673→  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674→    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675→    
   676→    // Check track capacity
   677→    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678→      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679→        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680→             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681→      );
   682→      
   683→      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684→        violations.push({
   685→          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686→          severity: 'HARD',
   687→          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688→        });
   689→      }
   690→    }
   691→    
   692→    // Check labor capacity
   693→    // Check material capacity
   694→    // ... similar validation logic
   695→    
   696→    return {
   697→      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698→      violations
   699→    };
   700→  }
   701→}
   702→```
   703→
   704→---
   705→
   706→## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707→
   708→### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709→
   710→Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711→
   712→- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713→- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714→- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715→- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716→
   717→### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718→
   719→The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720→
   721→- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722→- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723→- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724→- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725→
   726→### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727→
   728→For 12-year government contract compliance:
   729→
   730→- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731→- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732→- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733→- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734→
   735→### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736→
   737→Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738→
   739→- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740→- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741→- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742→- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743→

   197→#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
   198→```typescript
   199→// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
   200→import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';
   201→
   202→const TrainModel = createSchema({
   203→  id: 'string',
   204→  trainNumber: 'string',
   205→  mileageKm: 'number',
   206→  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
   207→  tags: 'string[]',
   208→  metadata: 'object?',
   209→});
   210→type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
   211→const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
   212→
   213→// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
   214→import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';
   215→
   216→const fleetSlice = createSlice({
   217→  id: 'fleet',
   218→  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
   219→  cache: { policy: 'session' },
   220→  reducers: {
   221→    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
   222→    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
   223→  },
   224→});
   225→
   226→const machine = createMachine({
   227→  id: 'mms',
   228→  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
   229→  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
   230→});
   231→
   232→// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
   233→const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
   234→```

# MMS Implementation Plan

> **Status**: Comprehensive plan generated from spec analysis (v2.1)
> **Last Updated**: 2026-01-19
> **Priority**: P1 (Critical) → P2 (High) → P3 (Medium) → P4 (Low)

---

## Current State Analysis

**Codebase Status**: Database Layer Complete (Phase 1.1)
- React 18 + Vite frontend shell (App.tsx, main.tsx)
- **Database schema**: 30+ tables across 14 schema files (Drizzle ORM)
  - Core Fleet: depots, fleets, trains, routes, tracks
  - Operational Constraints: service_demand, route_utilization_profiles, unavailability_events, train_route_compatibility
  - PM Management: pm_types, pm_material_requirements, pm_schedule
  - Mileage Tracking: mileage_readings, meter_events
  - Component Tracking: train_components, component_installations, component_pm_tracking
  - Material Management: inventory_positions, material_reservations
  - Man-hour Planning: manhour_capacity, manhour_projections, manhour_adjustments
  - Baseline & Drift: plan_baselines, baseline_pm_schedule, baseline_mileage_projection, drift_analysis
  - Scenarios: scenarios, scenario_deltas, scenario_comparisons
  - Costing: labor_rates, material_costs, cost_baselines
  - Audit & Auth: users, roles, sessions, pm_schedule_history, audit_log
- **Database utilities**: Connection pool with health checks (src/lib/db/connection.ts)
- **Drizzle config**: Migration configuration (drizzle.config.ts)
- **Manual migrations** (drizzle/manual/):
  - PostgreSQL extensions (uuid-ossp, timescaledb)
  - TimescaleDB hypertable (7-day chunks), compression (30 days), retention (13 years)
  - daily_fleet_mileage continuous aggregate
  - Database functions: train fitness sync, canvas refresh, scenario cleanup, reservation release, fleet targets, PM hierarchy, audit
  - Canvas views: v_canvas_strategic, v_canvas_tactical, v_manhour_canvas, v_drift_canvas
  - Development seed data
- Dependencies: drizzle-orm, postgres.js, pino, @belt/tools

**Next Priority**: Core Domain Services (Ingestion, Unavailability, Service Demand, Fleet Service)

**Critical Path**: ~~Database Schema~~ → ~~TimescaleDB Config~~ → ~~Shared Lib~~ → Domain Services → Optimizer → API → UI

---

## Phase 1: Foundation (P1 - Critical)

### 1.1 Database Schema & Migrations

#### Scenario Management (Event-Sourced)

#### Costing Domain

#### Audit & Auth Domain

#### Enums & Types

#### Database Functions & Triggers

#### State Machine Setup (for Phase 7 UI)

#### Reference: @belt/tools Patterns
```typescript
// Schema validation (replaces Zod)
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
  tags: 'string[]',
  metadata: 'object?',
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);

// State machine (replaces React hooks + zustand)
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
    selectTrain: (state, id) => { state.selected = id; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

## Notes

1. **Non-negotiable dependencies**: Corrective maintenance visibility and service demand are prerequisites for valid schedules
2. **Dual-solver architecture**: Strategic (GA) and tactical (OR-Tools CP-SAT) must share constraint envelope
3. **Explainability**: Every optimizer decision must include factor-based explanation traces
4. **Baseline/drift**: Long-term plans stabilize via published baselines; drift thresholds prevent jitter
5. **Material constraints**: PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless
6. **Cold-start risk**: Historical PM data will be incomplete - explicit confidence scoring required
7. **Audit compliance**: 12-year government contract mandates immutable audit trail with full attribution

# MMS Operational Guide

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Full stack (dev)
docker-compose up -d
bun run dev

# Tests
bun test                    # Unit tests
bun test:watch             # Watch mode
bun run test:integration   # Integration (needs Docker)

# Validation (run in order!)
bun run typecheck          # TypeScript strict
bun run lint               # ESLint
bun run build              # Full build
```

## Validation Order (Backpressure)

**IMPORTANT: Run these in order after implementing changes:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path-to-changed-file>

# 2. TypeScript type checking
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (if touching API/DB)
bun run test:integration
```

All must pass before committing.

## Build & Run

### Development Environment

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis minio

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Seed demo data (optional)
bun run db:seed

# Start dev server
bun run dev
```

### C++ Optimizer Build

```bash
# Prerequisites: cmake, or-tools
cd src/optimizer
mkdir build && cd build
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
make -j$(nproc)

# Binary location: src/optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
```

### Docker Full Stack

```bash
docker-compose -f docker-compose.full.yml up -d

# Services:
# - postgres:5432
# - redis:6379
# - minio:9000 (console: 9001)
# - api:8000
# - websocket:8001
# - frontend:3000
```

## Database

### Migrations
```bash
bun run db:migrate              # Run pending
bun run db:migrate:status       # Check status
bun run db:rollback             # Rollback last
```

### Drizzle Studio
```bash
bun run db:studio               # Opens at localhost:4983
```

### Direct Access
```bash
docker exec -it mms-postgres psql -U mms -d mms_dev
```

## Environment Variables

```bash
# Required
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms_dev
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
MINIO_ENDPOINT=localhost:9000
MINIO_ACCESS_KEY=mms
MINIO_SECRET_KEY=mms_secret

# Optional
LOG_LEVEL=debug
OPTIMIZER_PATH=./src/optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
MAXIMO_API_URL=http://maximo.example.com/api
```

## Codebase Patterns

### Domain Services
- Location: `src/services/`
- Pattern: Class with injected dependencies
- All services emit OpenTelemetry traces
- Use Drizzle ORM for database access

### API Routes
- Location: `src/api/routes/`
- Framework: Hono
- All routes have OpenAPI spec
- Use @belt/tools createSchema for validation

### Workers
- Location: `src/workers/`
- Framework: BullMQ
- Each worker is a separate file
- Concurrency defined in worker config

### Optimizer IPC
- Protocol: NDJSON over stdin/stdout
- Input: `{ type: "solve", data: {...} }`
- Output: `{ type: "progress" | "solution" | "error", ... }`

### Validation & State Management (@belt/tools)

> **CRITICAL**: See README.md for complete @belt/tools API reference

**Schema Validation** (replaces Zod):
```typescript
import { createSchema, createType, InferType } from '@belt/tools';

const TrainModel = createSchema({
  id: 'string',
  trainNumber: 'string',
  mileageKm: 'number',
  fitnessStatus: { enum: ['FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED'] },
});
type Train = InferType<typeof TrainModel>;
const train = TrainModel.parse(data);
```

**State Management** (replaces React hooks + zustand):
```typescript
import { createSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools';

const fleetSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'fleet',
  initial: { trains: [], selected: null },
  cache: { policy: 'session' },
  reducers: {
    setTrains: (state, trains) => { state.trains = trains; },
  },
});

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'mms',
  slices: { fleet: fleetSlice },
  trackMetadata: false, // Critical for 60fps
});

// In React component (NO useState/useEffect!)
const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('fleet');
```

### Forbidden Packages (enforced by .claude/hooks/)

> These packages are BLOCKED by .claude/hooks/block-forbidden-packages.sh

| Forbidden | Use Instead |
|-----------|-------------|
| zustand, redux, mobx, xstate | @belt/tools createMachine/createSlice |
| @mui, antd, @patternfly | @blueprintjs/core |
| tailwind | BlueprintJS styles |
| next.js | Vite SPA |

### React Hook Restrictions (enforced by .agentrc.ts)

> These React hooks are BLOCKED - use @belt/tools machine instead

- ❌ useState, useEffect, useReducer, useContext
- ❌ useCallback, useMemo, useLayoutEffect
- ❌ useTransition, useDeferredValue
- ✅ useRef (explicitly allowed)

## Common Issues

### Port conflicts
```bash
lsof -i :5432  # Check postgres
lsof -i :6379  # Check redis
```

### Database connection
```bash
# Reset completely
docker-compose down -v
docker-compose up -d postgres
bun run db:migrate
```

### Optimizer not found
```bash
# Check binary exists
ls -la src/optimizer/build/mms-optimizer

# Rebuild if needed
cd src/optimizer/build && make clean && make
```

   101→### 8.2.2 Container Images
   102→
   103→| Image | Base | Size Target | Purpose |
   104→|-------|------|-------------|---------|
   105→| `mms-frontend` | nginx:alpine | <50MB | Static React assets |
   106→| `mms-backend` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | API server |
   107→| `mms-worker` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | Background workers |
   108→| `mms-optimizer` | debian:slim | <500MB | C++ optimizer binary |
   109→| `mms-file-watcher` | oven/bun:alpine | <150MB | File ingestion daemon |
   110→
   111→### 8.2.3 Dockerfile Examples
   112→
   113→**Backend Dockerfile:**
   114→```dockerfile
   115→# Build stage
   116→FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS builder
   117→
   118→WORKDIR /app
   119→COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
   120→RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile
   121→
   122→COPY . .
   123→RUN bun run build
   124→
   125→# Production stage
   126→FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine
   127→
   128→WORKDIR /app
   129→
   130→# Add non-root user
   131→RUN addgroup -S mms && adduser -S mms -G mms
   132→USER mms
   133→
   134→COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
   135→COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
   136→COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
   137→
   138→ENV NODE_ENV=production
   139→ENV PORT=8000
   140→
   141→EXPOSE 8000
   142→
   143→HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
   144→  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1
   145→
   146→CMD ["bun", "run", "dist/server.js"]
   147→```
   148→
   149→**Optimizer Dockerfile:**
   150→```dockerfile
   151→# Build stage
   152→FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS builder
   153→
   154→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   155→  build-essential \
   156→  cmake \
   157→  git \
   158→  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   159→
   160→# Install OR-Tools
   161→RUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/google/or-tools.git /or-tools
   162→WORKDIR /or-tools
   163→RUN cmake -S . -B build -DBUILD_DEPS=ON
   164→RUN cmake --build build --config Release -j$(nproc)
   165→
   166→# Build optimizer
   167→WORKDIR /app
   168→COPY . .
   169→RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/or-tools/build
   170→RUN cmake --build build --config Release
   171→
   172→# Production stage
   173→FROM debian:bookworm-slim
   174→
   175→RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
   176→  libstdc++6 \
   177→  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
   178→
   179→# Add non-root user
   180→RUN useradd -r -s /bin/false mms
   181→USER mms
   182→

### 8.2.2 Container Images

| Image | Base | Size Target | Purpose |
|-------|------|-------------|---------|
| `mms-frontend` | nginx:alpine | <50MB | Static React assets |
| `mms-backend` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | API server |
| `mms-worker` | oven/bun:alpine | <200MB | Background workers |
| `mms-optimizer` | debian:slim | <500MB | C++ optimizer binary |
| `mms-file-watcher` | oven/bun:alpine | <150MB | File ingestion daemon |

### 8.2.3 Dockerfile Examples

**Backend Dockerfile:**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile

COPY . .
RUN bun run build

# Production stage
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine

WORKDIR /app

# Add non-root user
RUN addgroup -S mms && adduser -S mms -G mms
USER mms

COPY --from=builder /app/dist ./dist
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./

ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=8000

EXPOSE 8000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:8000/health || exit 1

CMD ["bun", "run", "dist/server.js"]
```

**Optimizer Dockerfile:**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM debian:bookworm-slim AS builder

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
  build-essential \
  cmake \
  git \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install OR-Tools
RUN git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/google/or-tools.git /or-tools
WORKDIR /or-tools
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DBUILD_DEPS=ON
RUN cmake --build build --config Release -j$(nproc)

# Build optimizer
WORKDIR /app
COPY . .
RUN cmake -S . -B build -DCMAKE_PREFIX_PATH=/or-tools/build
RUN cmake --build build --config Release

# Production stage
FROM debian:bookworm-slim

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
  libstdc++6 \
  && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Add non-root user
RUN useradd -r -s /bin/false mms
USER mms

WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/build/mms-optimizer .

ENTRYPOINT ["./mms-optimizer"]
```

---

## 8.3 Kubernetes Manifests

### 8.3.1 Backend Deployment

```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: mms-backend
  namespace: mms
  labels:
    app: mms
    component: backend
spec:
  replicas: 3
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1
      maxUnavailable: 0
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mms
      component: backend
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mms
        component: backend
      annotations:
        prometheus.io/scrape: "true"
        prometheus.io/port: "8000"
        prometheus.io/path: "/metrics"
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: mms-backend
      securityContext:
        runAsNonRoot: true
        runAsUser: 1000
        fsGroup: 1000
      containers:
        - name: backend
          image: registry.metro.example/mms/mms-backend:2.0.0
          imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: 8000
              protocol: TCP
          env:
            - name: NODE_ENV
              value: "production"
            - name: DATABASE_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets
                  key: database-url
            - name: REDIS_URL
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:
                  name: mms-secrets

Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile a final detailed report on the optimizer implementation status.

## Exploration Report: C++ OR-Tools Optimizer Implementation

### 1. Directory Structure and Current State

**src/optimizer/ directory:** EMPTY
- The optimizer directory exists but contains no files
- No C++, CMake, or Makefile present
- No TypeScript fallback optimizer implementation exists

### 2. Architecture Specifications (from spec 002-architecture.md)

The architecture defines a dual-path optimizer approach:

**Primary Path: C++ OR-Tools Binary**
- Binary name: `mms-optimizer`
- Location: `src/optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
- Technology: OR-Tools CP-SAT solver
- Communication: NDJSON over stdin/stdout
- Base image: debian:slim (<500MB)

**Fallback Path: TypeScript Greedy Solver**
- Triggers: C++ binary timeout, crash, or INFEASIBLE result
- Implementation: Simple greedy algorithm
- Status marking: Results marked with `status: 'FALLBACK'`

### 3. IPC Protocol Implementation (NDJSON Stream)

**Architecture:**
```
┌─────────────────┐    stdin (NDJSON)     ┌─────────────────┐
│   Bun Worker    │ ──────────────────►   │  mms-optimizer  │
│   (TypeScript)  │                       │     (C++)       │
│                 │ ◄────────────────────  │                 │
│                 │    stdout (NDJSON)     │                 │
│                 │ ◄────────────────────  │                 │
│                 │    stderr (logs)       │                 │
└─────────────────┘                       └─────────────────┘
```

**Input Message Types:**
- `SOLVE_REQUEST`: Main optimization request with trains, routes, constraints

**Output Message Types:**
- `HEARTBEAT`: Every 5 seconds during solve (memory_mb, thread_count, branches_per_second)
- `PROGRESS`: Periodic updates (percent, current_objective, feasible, elapsed_ms)
- `RESULT`: Final solution with assignments and explanations
- `ERROR`: Error conditions with recovery suggestions

**Heartbeat Monitoring:**
- Interval: 5 seconds from C++ binary
- Timeout: 15 seconds (3 missed heartbeats) triggers SIGKILL
- Purpose: Detect hung processes, segfaults, infinite loops

### 4. Constraint Handling (Hard vs Soft)

**Hard Constraints (MUST NOT violate):**
1. Each train assigned to ≤1 route
2. Route requirements (exactly N trains)
3. Fleet compatibility
4. Fitness status (FIT/RESTRICTED/GROUNDED)
5. Unavailability events (CM, incidents)
6. Route compatibility matrix
7. Service demand minimums (CRITICAL)
8. PM due today (blocks assignment)
9. Material unavailability for blocking parts

**Soft Constraints (minimize violations with penalties):**
1. Mileage imbalance (weight: 100)
2. Route preference (weight: 50)
3. Overtime hours (weight: 200)
4. Depot preference
5. Material availability warnings (90-day window)

**Penalty Calculation:**
```cpp
// Objective: Minimize total penalty
model.Minimize(LinearExpr::Sum(mileage_penalties));
```

### 5. TypeScript Fallback Optimizer (from spec 004-domain-services.md)

**Implementation Pattern (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED):**
```typescript
class RunAssignmentOptimizer {
  private async runFallbackOptimizer(input: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerResult> {
    // 1. Sort routes by priority (required trains desc)
    // 2. Sort trains by fitness and mileage balance
    // 3. Greedy assignment with hard constraint checks
    // 4. Return with status: 'FALLBACK' and high penalty (1e6)
  }
}
```

**Fallback Triggers:**
1. C++ binary crash (exit code ≠ 0)
2. Process timeout (default 60 seconds)
3. INFEASIBLE result from CP-SAT
4. Heartbeat timeout (15 seconds)

**Greedy Algorithm:**
- Sort routes by required_trains descending
- Sort trains by mileage balance (under-utilized first)
- Iterate and assign respecting hard constraints only
- Mark result with high penalty to indicate suboptimal

### 6. Comparison Against Specifications

**spec 002-architecture.md Section 2.2.5 (C++ Optimizer Binary):**

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------|
| OR-Tools CP-SAT solver | ❌ Not implemented | No C++ code exists |
| Soft constraint penalties | ❌ Not implemented | Specified in spec only |
| Explanation generation | ❌ Not implemented | Factor-based trace required |
| Progress streaming | ❌ Not implemented | NDJSON to stdout |
| Metrics emission | ❌ Not implemented | OpenTelemetry to stderr |
| Fallback mode | ❌ Not implemented | Greedy heuristic |
| Material validation | ❌ Not implemented | Inventory check logic |

**spec 004-domain-services.md Section 4.3 (Run Assignment Optimizer):**

| Component | Status | Location |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| OptimizerService (TypeScript wrapper) | ❌ Not implemented | Should be in src/services/ |
| C++ optimizer binary | ❌ Not implemented | src/optimizer/ is empty |
| Fallback optimizer | ❌ Not implemented | Greedy algorithm |
| BullMQ optimizer_worker | ❌ Not implemented | src/workers/ is empty |
| IPC protocol handlers | ❌ Not implemented | stdin/stdout/stderr |
| Heartbeat monitoring | ❌ Not implemented | 5s interval, 15s timeout |
| Signal handlers (C++) | ❌ Not implemented | SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE |

### 7. Build System Requirements (from spec 008-operations.md)

**CMakeLists.txt Structure (NOT PRESENT):**
```cmake
# Expected structure:
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.20)
project(mms-optimizer)
find_package(ortools REQUIRED)
add_executable(mms-optimizer src/solver.cpp)
target_link_libraries(mms-optimizer ortools::ortools)
```

**Build Commands (from ralph/AGENTS.md):**

mkdir build && cd build
cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
make -j$(nproc)
```

**Docker Build (Specified but not present):**
- Multi-stage: debian:bookworm-slim
- OR-Tools: Git clone and build
- Binary copy to production stage
- Size target: <500MB

### 8. Implementation Gaps

**Phase 3.1 (C++ Run Assignment Optimizer) - ALL MISSING:**
- [ ] CMake build configuration
- [ ] OR-Tools dependency setup
- [ ] NDJSON input parsing

### 9. Related Implementations

**Existing Services with Optimizer Dependencies:**
- `PMSchedulerService` (src/services/pm-scheduler.ts): Implements tactical scheduling but without optimizer integration
- `ServiceDemandService` (src/services/service-demand.ts): Provides hard constraint validation
